commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds shared sanitizer/normalizer rules and regression tests to strip copied current-message/history scaffolding and standalone `(no output)` placeholders while preserving non-text payloads.

PR surface: Source +69, Tests +78. Total +147 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level. Current main routes replies through `normalizeReplyPayload` before delivery and lacks rules for the reported scaffold strings and standalone `(no output)` placeholder.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can constrain the over-broad sanitizer condition and add one regression test on the PR branch.

target.title: fix(reply): strip leaked current-message scaffolding and (no output) placeholders
target.body:
Fixes #78177.

## Summary
Final user-facing replies could surface internal scaffolding when the model echoed its own input back into the reply text:

- the prompt headers `[Chat messages since your last reply - for context]`, `[Current message - respond to this]`, and `Current message priority: ŌĆ”`
- bare `(no output)` tool placeholders

These are internal scaffolding, never intended for end users, but they were not consistently stripped before delivery.

## Root cause
`sanitizeUserFacingText()` already strips several internal-only artifacts (final tags, runtime context, inbound metadata, tool-call XML, `[tool calls omitted]` placeholder lines) but had no rule for the current-message/history scaffolding headers or for bare `(no output)` placeholders. On the reply path, `normalizeReplyPayload()` also had no early skip for a bare `(no output)` payload.

## Changes
- **`src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.ts`**
  - `stripPlaceholderLines` (renamed from `stripToolCallsOmittedPlaceholderLines`) now also drops standalone `(no output)` lines, mirroring the existing `[tool calls omitted]` rule.
  - New `stripCopiedCurrentMessageScaffolding` removes standalone scaffolding blocks whose first line is one of the three markers, while preserving ordinary inline prose that merely mentions the phrase.
- **`src/auto-reply/reply/normalize-reply.ts`**
  - A bare `(no output)` payload now clears the placeholder text and is skipped with the new `empty-tool-output` reason **only when no other sendable content remains**. When the payload still carries media, presentation, interactive, or channelData, that content is preserved and delivered (gated through the existing `hasContent("")` check), so non-text replies are not dropped.
- Regression tests added for the sanitizer (`embedded-agent-helpers.sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts`) and the normalizer (`reply-utils.test.ts`), including coverage that a bare `(no output)` payload carrying media/channelData still delivers its non-text content.

All rules match standalone lines/blocks only, so text that merely mentions the literal strings inline is preserved ŌĆö see the `"The literal (no output) text is relevant."` case.

> This re-authors the approach from #78189 (which was closed only for an author PR-queue limit, not on review/design grounds) against current `main`: the helper moved from `pi-embedded-helpers/` to `embedded-agent-helpers/`, and the sanitize pipeline gained `stripAssistantInternalTraceLines` / `stripPlainTextToolCallBlocks` steps since then, so the change is rebased onto the current shape.

## Testing
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-utils.test.ts` ŌåÆ **121 + 76 passing** (including the new cases)
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental false` ŌåÆ clean
- `oxlint` and `oxfmt --check` on the changed files ŌåÆ clean

## AI-assisted disclosure
This PR was prepared with AI assistance. Per `CONTRIBUTING.md`, the automated tests/typecheck/lint above are supplemental. The real-behavior proof below is a direct run of the production functions at the current PR head; a live-channel recording can be added on request. `CHANGELOG.md` intentionally left untouched per the contributor guidelines.


## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Final user-facing replies could leak internal prompt scaffolding ŌĆö `[Chat messages since your last reply - for context]`, `[Current message - respond to this]`, `Current message priority: ŌĆ”` ŌĆö and bare `(no output)` tool placeholders when the model echoed its own input back into the reply text; additionally, the reply normalizer must not drop a media/channelData payload whose text is a bare `(no output)` (#78177).

**Real environment tested:** Clean checkout at the current PR head `ef8553bc` on Windows 11, Node `v24.13.0`. `git rev-parse HEAD` was verified to equal `ef8553bccc68ŌĆ”` with a clean working tree immediately before and after the run. The shipped `sanitizeUserFacingText()` (`src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.ts`) and `normalizeReplyPayload()` (`src/auto-reply/reply/normalize-reply.ts`) ŌĆö the same functions the reply pipeline calls before delivery ŌĆö were executed directly against the issue inputs and against `(no output)` payloads carrying non-text content. This is a direct run of the production code paths at current head; it is not a full live-channel deployment (happy to add a live channel recording if preferred).

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** A harness imports the real exported functions and prints input/output (and skip reasons):

```ts
import { sanitizeUserFacingText } from "./src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.js";
import { normalizeReplyPayload } from "./src/auto-reply/reply/normalize-reply.js";
// sanitizeUserFacingText(...) on the scaffolding / (no output) inputs
// normalizeReplyPayload(payload, { onSkip }) on bare (no output), +mediaUrl, +channelData
```

Command: `node --import tsx _proof_94112.mts` (executed with `HEAD == ef8553bc`, tree clean).

**Evidence after fix:** Copied console output from the current-head (`ef8553bc`) run:

```text
===== sanitizeUserFacingText (current-message / history scaffolding) =====
IN : "Current message priority: high\n[Current message - respond to this]\n[Telegram 2026-05-05T20:20:00Z] Danny: ping\n[from: Danny]\n\nPong."
OUT: "Pong."
IN : "[Chat messages since your last reply - for context]\n[Telegram 2026-05-05T20:19:00Z] Danny: previous\n\n[Current message - respond to this]\n[Telegram 2026-05-05T20:20:00Z] Danny: current\n\nVisible reply."
OUT: "Visible reply."

===== sanitizeUserFacingText ((no output) placeholder) =====
IN : "(no output)"                                  OUT: ""
IN : "Before\n(no output)\nAfter"                   OUT: "Before\nAfter"
IN : "The literal (no output) text is relevant."    OUT: "The literal (no output) text is relevant."

===== normalizeReplyPayload ((no output) skip vs non-text preservation) =====
--- bare (no output) -> skipped
  payload : {"text":"(no output)"}
  result  : null
  skips   : ["empty-tool-output"]
--- (no output) + mediaUrl -> media kept, text cleared
  payload : {"text":"  (no output)\n","mediaUrl":"https://example.com/photo.jpg"}
  result  : {"text":"","mediaUrl":"https://example.com/photo.jpg"}
  skips   : []
--- (no output) + channelData -> channelData kept, text cleared
  payload : {"text":"(no output)","channelData":{"line":{"flexMessage":{"type":"bubble"}}}}
  result  : {"text":"","channelData":{"line":{"flexMessage":{"type":"bubble"}}}}
  skips   : []
```

**Observed result after fix:** At current head `ef8553bc`, `sanitizeUserFacingText` strips the `Current message priority:` / `[Current message - respond to this]` / `[Chat messages since your last reply - for context]` scaffolding and standalone `(no output)` lines, returning only the genuine reply (`Pong.`, `Visible reply.`, `Before\nAfter`), while the inline mention (`The literal (no output) text is relevant.`) is returned unchanged. `normalizeReplyPayload` skips a bare `(no output)` payload (result `null`, reason `empty-tool-output`); when the same `(no output)` payload also carries `mediaUrl` or `channelData`, the placeholder text is cleared to `""` and the non-text content is preserved and delivered (no skip recorded). Current `main` / `v2026.6.8` lack these sanitizer/normalizer rules, so the identical inputs surface the scaffolding and `(no output)` verbatim.

**What was not tested:** A full end-to-end delivery through a live messaging channel (e.g., a real Telegram bot) was not exercised ŌĆö verification runs the production functions directly against the issue inputs and payloads.
target.author: snowsky1

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
